skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos with the RunComfy CLI. The skill selects a RunComfy model route for portrait audio sync, script-driven video, stylized character animation, or cinematic reference-based avatar clips.
skill.summary: Create RunComfy avatar and lip-sync videos from portraits, scripts, audio files, and reference media.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk-looking alerts, but most Ruby backtick, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance matches are Markdown formatting or prose false positives. Confirmed residual risk comes from legitimate RunComfy CLI execution, third-party network transfer of media assets, and local API token storage. No evidence found of prompt injection, malware intent, credential exfiltration, or arbitrary shell execution beyond the documented runcomfy command scope.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run RunComfy CLI commands with user-supplied model inputs. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes an external command and depends on correct JSON argument handling.
medium_findings.recommendation: Keep the Bash allowlist limited to runcomfy commands, preserve JSON argument boundaries, and do not add arbitrary shell pipelines or remote installer execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains repeated runcomfy command examples and states the skill invokes runcomfy run. The commands are expected for this skill, so this is a controlled operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Network Transfer of Media Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill routes portrait images, audio URLs, and generated outputs through RunComfy services. This can expose user media and voice assets to a third-party service even though the endpoints are documented and allowlisted.
medium_findings.recommendation: Warn users before sending sensitive media to RunComfy, require consent for likeness and voice assets, and keep outbound endpoints constrained to RunComfy domains.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly says the CLI posts to the Model API, polls status, and downloads RunComfy URLs. The privacy impact is clear, but the network use matches the advertised functionality.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy Token Stored in Hidden Config File
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login stores an API token under the user home directory. This is a normal CLI credential pattern, but marketplace users should understand that a local bearer token is created unless RUNCOMFY_TOKEN is used.
medium_findings.recommendation: Ensure token files remain user-readable only, document revocation steps, and prefer RUNCOMFY_TOKEN in disposable CI or container environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and file mode are directly documented on the flagged line. This confirms credential storage, but there is no evidence of token exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby backtick execution findings, but the reviewed file is Markdown. The flagged backticks are inline code spans, model identifiers, or fenced command examples rather than Ruby code.
low_findings.recommendation: Treat Markdown code formatting separately from executable source code during static analysis. Review the actual allowed Bash scope instead.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged file is SKILL.md and the suspicious syntax appears inside prose, links, and fenced examples. No Ruby source file or executable backtick expression was found.

low_findings.title: Textual False Positives for Crypto and Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts match ordinary prose such as AI model descriptions, route guidance, and exit-code documentation. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, host probing, or reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these specific static matches unless executable code is added. Continue reviewing future changes for real cryptographic or host inspection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown metadata, model selection advice, and a documentation table. They do not contain hashing calls, encryption choices, or system inspection commands.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast avatar videos without choosing the wrong lip-sync model. This skill selects the RunComfy route and provides the right CLI inputs for the job.
actual_capabilities: Selects among OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, Wan 2-2 Animate, HappyHorse, and Seedance v2 Pro routes.
Provides RunComfy CLI commands for portrait-to-video, audio-driven lip-sync, and script-driven clips.
Explains when to use external audio, quoted spoken scripts, reference images, and reference audio.
Documents prompt patterns for UGC ads, product demos, stylized mascots, and cinematic monologues.
Includes privacy guidance for consent, token storage, shell boundaries, and outbound RunComfy endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy login or token.
Most routes require externally hosted image or audio URLs.
Generated quality depends on portrait framing, audio clarity, and model availability.
Users must have rights and consent for every likeness and voice asset.

use_cases.title: Produce UGC Product Ads
use_cases.description: Turn a portrait and clean voiceover into a short vertical presenter clip for product launches or paid social tests.

use_cases.title: Localize Presenter Videos
use_cases.description: Reuse the same approved portrait with different language audio tracks to create consistent dubbed avatar clips.

use_cases.title: Animate Characters and Monologues
use_cases.description: Choose stylized character animation or cinematic reference-based generation when a simple portrait avatar is not enough.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Talking Head
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video from this portrait URL and voiceover URL. Choose the simplest RunComfy route and provide the command.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Script-Driven Avatar Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: I only have this short script and need an avatar video. Select a route that can generate speech during the video pass.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Lip-Sync Route for a Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this voiceover URL and describe a studio scene for the avatar. Pick the route that gives scene control and audio-driven mouth sync.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Cinematic Reference Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a RunComfy plan for a cinematic monologue using a subject image, reference audio, aspect ratio, duration, lighting, and camera direction.

output_examples.input: A marketer provides a portrait and a product voiceover for a short social ad.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends OmniHuman, explains why the portrait and audio route fits, and returns a RunComfy command with the needed media fields.

output_examples.input: A creator has only a written line for a mascot character.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends HappyHorse or Wan 2-2 Animate based on the character style and explains how to place the spoken line in the prompt.

output_examples.input: A producer wants a polished monologue with subject, audio, and cinematic references.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends Seedance v2 Pro and outlines the reference media roles, duration, aspect ratio, and prompt direction.

best_practices: Use clean voice audio and a clear portrait with head-and-shoulders framing when possible.
Confirm consent and usage rights before pairing a real person's face with any voice track.
Choose the simplest model route that satisfies the audio, style, and camera requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not use a real person's likeness or voice without permission.
Do not paste unescaped prompt text into shell commands outside the documented JSON input boundary.
Do not use a cinematic multi-reference route for a simple portrait-and-audio lip-sync task.

faq.question: Does this skill create videos directly?
faq.answer: It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to run the RunComfy CLI with the correct model and inputs.

faq.question: Do I need an audio file?
faq.answer: OmniHuman, Wan, and Seedance routes use audio files. HappyHorse can create speech from a quoted script.

faq.question: Can it animate stylized characters?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill points stylized or illustrated characters toward the Wan 2-2 Animate route.

faq.question: Where are media files sent?
faq.answer: The workflow sends model requests and media URLs to RunComfy services, then downloads generated outputs.

faq.question: Is it safe for real people?
faq.answer: Use it only with consent for the person's likeness and voice. The skill includes guidance to refuse harmful synthetic media.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
